output.summary: Review of takt/header block against block-type requirements. Found 1 critical bug (JS breakpoint mismatch), 2 major bugs (TSX/PHP nav list class sync, missing aria-label on logo in TSX), and several minor issues. Test page needs update to use DemoContainer wrappers per new standards.

fileChanges.description: Fix media query breakpoint from 48rem (768px) to 60rem (960px) to match theme's md breakpoint
fileChanges.details: Line 4: Change `window.matchMedia('(min-width: 48rem)')` to `window.matchMedia('(min-width: 60rem)')`. The theme defines --breakpoint-md as 60rem (960px) in spacing.css. The current 48rem value means JS considers 768-959px as 'desktop' while CSS treats it as 'mobile' via max-md: prefix, causing scroll behavior, menu state, and inert attribute mismatches.

fileChanges.description: Sync nav list UL classes with PHP version for responsive layout
fileChanges.details: Line 128: The innerBlocksProps className is `flex gap-8 items-start justify-end flex-wrap list-none p-0 m-0` but PHP line 140 uses `flex md:flex-row md:gap-8 md:items-start md:justify-end md:flex-wrap max-md:flex-col max-md:gap-8 max-md:w-full list-none p-0 m-0`. The PHP has correct responsive classes for vertical mobile layout. Update TSX to match PHP: `flex md:flex-row md:gap-8 md:items-start md:justify-end md:flex-wrap max-md:flex-col max-md:gap-8 max-md:w-full list-none p-0 m-0`.

fileChanges.description: Add aria-label to logo link in TSX to match PHP
fileChanges.details: Line 599-604: TSX logo link has `aria-label={__('Home', 'takt')}` but PHP has `aria-label="{blogname} - Home"`. For better accessibility, update TSX to construct a similar label. However, since TSX is the editor preview and PHP is the frontend, this is a sync concern but not a runtime issue. The PHP version is more descriptive. Recommend keeping PHP pattern and noting the TSX limitation (no access to bloginfo in editor). This is a minor sync difference that does not affect the frontend.

fileChanges.description: Fix desktop right column conditional rendering to always show when search or sep link exist
fileChanges.details:
Line 67: PHP always renders the desktop right column `
...
` unconditionally, but TSX (line 611) wraps it in `{(displayContent || hasSearchLink || hasSepLink) && (...)}`. Since the search form is always useful (users can type even without a configured URL), the PHP approach of always rendering is actually correct. The TSX should match PHP by removing the conditional. However, the PHP always shows the search even when there's no URL, which falls back to '/' -- this is acceptable. No change needed on PHP side, but TSX should be updated to always show the desktop right column for consistency.

fileChanges.description: Always render desktop right column to match PHP (remove conditional wrapper)
fileChanges.details: Lines 611-694: Remove the `{(displayContent || hasSearchLink || hasSepLink) && (...)}` conditional wrapper around the desktop right column. The PHP always renders it. The search form and SEP link inside it already have their own conditionals.

fileChanges.description: Add Enter/Space key support for submenu toggle buttons per Menu Button pattern
fileChanges.details: The submenu buttons already respond to click events which handle Enter/Space natively for <button> elements. However, the Menu Button pattern also requires: (1) Down Arrow opens menu and focuses first item, (2) Up Arrow opens menu and focuses last item. Currently Down/Up Arrow only work AFTER the menu is open. Add keyboard handler on the button itself to open the menu on ArrowDown/ArrowUp when the menu is closed.

fileChanges.description: Add Home/End key support within open submenus per Menu pattern
fileChanges.details: The current submenu keyboard navigation supports ArrowUp, ArrowDown, Escape, and Tab. The Menu and Menubar pattern also requires Home (first item) and End (last item) key support. Add these to the handleSubmenuKeydown function.

fileChanges.description: Add aria-label to submenu container for accessibility
fileChanges.details: Line 66: The submenu `<div id='...' role='menu'>` lacks an aria-label. Add `aria-label` with the parent item's title (e.g., 'About submenu') to provide context for screen reader users.

fileChanges.description: Add aria-label to submenu container to match PHP fix
fileChanges.details: Line 172-183: Add `aria-label` to the submenu div using the parent item's link title.

designDecisions.question: Should the desktop right column (search + SEP link) always render or only when search/SEP link is configured?
designDecisions.recommendation: Always render, matching current PHP behavior. The search form is useful even without a configured URL (falls back to '/'). TSX should be updated to match.

designDecisions.question: The JS breakpoint uses 48rem (768px) while the CSS md breakpoint is 60rem (960px). Which is correct?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use 60rem to match the theme's md breakpoint. The CSS uses md: prefix throughout, so JS must agree on when desktop/mobile transitions occur.

accessibilityGaps.description: JS breakpoint mismatch (48rem vs 60rem) causes inert attribute and menu state to be incorrectly applied in the 768-959px range. At these widths, CSS shows the mobile layout (max-md:) but JS treats it as desktop, so mobile menu toggle and inert states are wrong.

Submenu toggle buttons lack ArrowDown/ArrowUp keyboard support when menu is closed. WAI-ARIA Menu Button pattern requires Down Arrow to open menu and focus first item, Up Arrow to open menu and focus last item.
accessibilityGaps.fix: Add keydown event listener on submenu buttons in HeaderMain.js that handles ArrowDown (open + focus first) and ArrowUp (open + focus last) when aria-expanded is false.

accessibilityGaps.description: Submenu keyboard navigation missing Home/End key support. Menu pattern requires Home to focus first item and End to focus last item.
accessibilityGaps.fix: Add Home and End key handlers to handleSubmenuKeydown in HeaderMain.js.

accessibilityGaps.description: Submenu div[role='menu'] lacks aria-label. Screen readers cannot identify which navigation section the menu belongs to.
accessibilityGaps.fix: Add aria-label attribute to submenu container in both HeaderMainItem.tsx and HeaderMainItem.php using the parent item's title (e.g., 'About submenu').

accessibilityGaps.description: Mobile search form within the collapsible nav has role='search' which is correct, but the desktop search form also has role='search'. Having two search landmarks on the page is valid but could be confusing. Consider adding distinguishing aria-label values.
accessibilityGaps.fix: Add aria-label='Desktop search' to the desktop search form and aria-label='Mobile search' to the mobile search form, or use a single label like 'Site search' on both since only one is visible at a time.

accessibilityGaps.description: The nav element has tabindex='-1' which is unconventional. While it allows programmatic focus, the nav landmark itself should not be focusable. This was likely added for focus management when opening the mobile menu.
accessibilityGaps.fix: This is a minor concern and can remain as-is since it only allows programmatic focus (not tab focus). No change required.

scenarios: Full Content - All fields populated with logo, search, SEP link, mobile CTA, multiple nav items with submenus, fixed on scroll
Minimal Content - Logo and 2 simple nav items only, no SEP link, no search URL, no mobile CTA
Hide on Scroll Down (Desktop + Mobile) - Both desktop and mobile hide-on-scroll-down enabled
Hide on Scroll Down (Desktop Only) - Desktop hides on scroll down, mobile stays fixed
Not Fixed on Scroll - No fixed positioning at all, header scrolls with page
Submenu Alignment Right - Tests right-aligned dropdown submenu positioning
Many Nav Items (Overflow) - 8 items to test flex-wrap behavior
Long Submenu Items - Very long sub-item titles to test text wrapping
SEP Link Only (No Search URL) - SEP link configured but search URL is empty
currentIssues: Test page uses plain h2 headings instead of DemoContainer wrappers
Test page uses core/separator blocks between variations instead of DemoContainer's built-in separation
Title already follows correct pattern 'TEST: Header'
Missing scenario for SEP link without search URL
Missing scenario for both desktop and mobile hide-on-scroll-down

interactiveTests.name: Submenu opens on click
interactiveTests.expect: Submenu dropdown becomes visible, aria-expanded changes to true, chevron rotates 180deg

interactiveTests.name: Mobile menu opens on toggle click
interactiveTests.expect: Navigation panel expands, menu-open class added, toggle icon changes to close icon, aria-expanded becomes true

interactiveTests.name: Mobile menu closes on toggle click
interactiveTests.expect: Navigation panel collapses, menu-open class removed, aria-expanded becomes false
